title: How we govern a design system at scale
content:
## The challenge of scale

A design system that serves dozens of product teams across an organisation faces a different class of problems than one maintained by a single team. Decisions that seem straightforward in isolation — should a new component be added? should this token be changed? — can have far-reaching consequences when hundreds of engineers and designers depend on the system.

### Our contribution model

Green operates on an open contribution model with a core team responsible for governance. Any product team can propose new components or changes, but proposals must go through a structured review process covering design quality, accessibility, API design, and maintenance cost.

### Versioning and stability

We follow semantic versioning strictly. Patch releases fix bugs without API changes. Minor releases add new features in a backwards-compatible way. Major releases are allowed to introduce breaking changes, but we provide codemods and a migration guide for each one.

### The design-to-code handoff

One of the most significant investments we've made is in closing the design-to-code gap. Figma variables map directly to CSS custom properties. Component variants in Figma correspond to component props in code. When a designer changes a token in Figma, the change propagates automatically to the production token set via our CI pipeline.
